canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Traffic is consistently treated as a standard benchmark dataset containing time series data of road traffic volume records. Its most common application is to evaluate model performance on the task of time series forecasting, with one paper specifically noting its use for long-term forecasting models. Beyond general forecasting ability, the dataset is also used to measure the construct of knowledge transferability. The source snippets show that Traffic is frequently employed as part of a larger suite of time series datasets—including ETTm1, ETTm2, Electricity, and Weather—for both model evaluation and, as one paper mentions, for pre-training.
papers: TEMPO: Prompt-based Generative Pre-trained Transformer for Time Series Forecasting
Time-LLM: Time Series Forecasting by Reprogramming Large Language Models
LangTime: A Language-Guided Unified Model for Time Series Forecasting with Proximal Policy Optimization
Lightweight Online Adaption for Time Series Foundation Model Forecasts

definitions.text: A time series dataset containing road traffic volume records, used for evaluating forecasting performance across domains.
